stem: Two lines intersect at exactly one point, forming two acute angles and two obtuse angles. The measure of one of these angles is (7x − 220)°. Which of the following could NOT be the sum of the measures of any two of these angles?
rationale: When two lines intersect, opposite angles are congruent and adjacent angles are supplementary. With one angle measuring (7x − 220)°, the four angles are (7x − 220)°, (7x − 220)°, (180 − (7x − 220))°, and (180 − (7x − 220))°. Possible sums of pairs include: two acute (or two obtuse) of the same measure giving 2(7x − 220) = 14x − 440, or 2(400 − 7x) = 800 − 14x; and one acute + one obtuse adjacent giving 180°. The expression −14x + 440 = −(14x − 440), which is the negation of the two-acute sum and cannot equal any actual sum of two of these angles for all x.
